items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: Why did Henry VIII break with Rome?
items.correct: Primarily for an annulment from Catherine of Aragon, but also to seize Church wealth and assert royal supremacy — not for Protestant theological reasons.
items.misconception: Because he wanted England to become Protestant.
items.random_wrong: Because the Pope excommunicated him for marrying Anne Boleyn.
items.why: Henry remained doctrinally Catholic to his death. The break was political and dynastic; Protestantism only took hold in England under Edward VI and Elizabeth I.

items.q: Why was the NHS founded in 1948?
items.correct: Beveridge's 1942 report identified disease as a 'giant evil'; Labour's 1945 manifesto promised universal healthcare; Bevan unified hospitals and made care free at point of use.
items.misconception: The NHS was founded because Churchill wanted to thank soldiers after WWII.
items.random_wrong: The NHS was founded in 1918 after the influenza pandemic.
items.why: Churchill's Conservatives opposed parts of the NHS; it was Labour's Bevan who pushed it through. Students misattribute it to wartime consensus alone, missing the political conflict.

items.q: Who led the Bolshevik seizure of power in October 1917?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Lenin. “Stalin” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: France's pre-1789 monarchy. “Russia's secret police” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which year was the American Declaration of Independence signed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1776. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
